data.narrative:
Aerospacelab is a Series B space & aerospace company that has raised €200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift) alongside a broader public comparable universe of 5 space & aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 additional private peers for context.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. The three identified comparables valued at $1.3B, $400M, and $274M yield a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied; the public space & aerospace sector trades at a median 49.8x P/S across the 5 tickers, but Aerospacelab's revenue is not available in filed data, precluding revenue-multiple extension. Discounted cash flow analysis would require forward projections and margin assumptions similarly absent from disclosed information.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M, derived entirely from the peer-median method. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three private comparables—which span a wide $1.0B range—and the absence of revenue or profitability data to cross-validate against public multiples or alternative scaling metrics. The narrow applicability of a single method reflects limited publicly available financial disclosure in the frontier aerospace sector.
